reasoning: The question is set in a simulated 2025 where BTC is trading around $110,000 to $115,000 in early November. A dip to $90,000 represents an ~18-20% drop over the remaining 50 days of the year. On October 30, the implied probability was 25%. A search of news between Oct 30 and Nov 9, 2025, shows no massive crash (one article mentions a 6.5% drop), so BTC remains relatively elevated. Without a precipitous drop recently, and with the time decay reducing the window for a crash, the probability should be slightly lower or stable compared to the Oct 30 baseline. Based on typical crypto volatility and the earlier Polymarket price, the probability is evaluated at ~23%.
